title: CREED data
text: Criteria Reporting Evaluating Exposure Datasets (CREED) framework evaluating relevance reliability reference object given exposure assessment question across series questions. Users set criteria relevant question, use pre-defined set reliability criteria. object evaluated based data availability, precision, choice methods, final score calculated relevance reliability based useability “Gold” “Silver” levels quality. table collects relevant data non-CREED tables selected user automated data population process, user-appraised scores, limitations user chooses comments . data export process, functions used create two .CSV files: one reliability one relevance. Scores used calculate CREED score. table intended used final data product, included format provides traceability CREED score calculated. large part evaluation logic included eData app, table currently just container little relevant information. planned migrate logic eDataDRF future.

title: CREED data
text: criterion_id - string, free, mandatory alphanumeric serial identifier CREED’s relevance (RV1 - RV11) reliability (RB1 - RB19) criteria. criterion_title - string, free, mandatory short, descriptive title domain criterion, e.g. “Sample Medium/Matrix”. required_recommended - string, free, mandatory Whether criterion required (case used calculate silver score), recommended (gold score). relevant_data - string, free, mandatory relevant data provided auto-population process user. example: score - string, free, mandatory assessment score based extent data object fulfills criterion. Criterion scores stored named vector numbers. lower score (1) better, higher score (4) worse. limitations - string, free, mandatory limitations, caveats, relevant comments identified user conducting assessment.

title: Measurements Table
text: SITE_CODE - string, free, mandatory Site Code foreign key references Sites data table. Samples table creation user selects relevant sites using code name. PARAMETER_NAME - string, free, mandatory Parameter name foreign key references Parameter data table. Samples table creation user selects relevant parameters using name type. SAMPLING_DATE - string, free, mandatory Transcribed Sampling Date Samples data. ENVIRON_COMPARTMENT_SUB - string, controlled, mandatory Transcribed Environmental Sub-Compartment Samples data table. SUBSAMPLE - string, free, mandatory Transcribed Subsample Samples data table. MEASURED_FLAG - string, controlled, mandatory flag indicate data source reported measured value (blank) limit detection limit quantification. latter cases, Measured value becomes optional, mandatory complete least one LOQ/LOD. MEASURED_VALUE - numeric, free, conditionally mandatory numeric measured value parameter, available. UNCERTAINTY_TYPE - string, controlled, mandatory Many Measured values reported aggregated statistics uncertainty bounds (mean, mode, etc; 95% CI, SD, SE, range, etc.). Uncertainty Type provides non-exhaustive classification system . data source reports uncertaintly, Upper Lower also reported. UNCERTAINTY_UPPER - numeric, free, conditionally mandatory upper bound reported uncertainty. UNCERTAINTY_LOWER - numeric, free, conditionally mandatory lower bound reported uncertainty. MEASURED_UNIT - string, controlled, conditionally mandatory unit measurement associated data. non-exhaustive list options conversion factors provided part format. Mandatory Measured value provided. MEASURED_N - numeric, free, mandatory sample size biota population /number replicates Measured value represents. LOQ_VALUE - numeric, free, conditionally mandatory Limit Quantification method, reported. Measured value reported, least one LOQ LOD reported value unit; implicitly stressor occurrence method’s ability quantify. Measured value, LOQ /LOD reported, please include many possible extracted data. LOQ_UNIT - string, controlled, conditionally mandatory Unit associated LOQ Value Measured value reported, least one LOQ LOD reported value unit. Uses Measured Unit vocabulary. LOD_VALUE - numeric, free, conditionally mandatory Limit Detection method, reported. Measured value reported, least one LOQ LOD reported value unit; implicitly stressor occurrence method’s ability detect. Measured value, LOQ /LOD reported, please include many possible extracted data. LOD_UNIT - string, controlled, conditionally mandatory Unit associated LOD Value Measured value reported, least one LOQ LOD reported value unit. Uses Measured Unit vocabulary. SAMPLING_PROTOCOL - string, controlled, mandatory ID sampling protocol used generate measured sample. Foreign key referencing protocol ID. Limited sampling protocols. EXTRACTION_PROTOCOL - string, controlled, mandatory ID extraction protocol used generate measured sample. Foreign key referencing protocol ID. Limited extraction protocols. FRACTIONATION_PROTOCOL - string, controlled, mandatory ID fractionation protocol used generate measured sample. Foreign key referencing protocol ID. Limited fractionation protocols. ANALYTICAL_PROTOCOL - string, controlled, mandatory ID analytical protocol used generate measured sample. Foreign key referencing protocol ID. Limited analytical protocols. REFERENCE_ID - string, free, mandatory Identifier data source, foreign key referencing reference id. SAMPLE_ID - string, free, mandatory CAMPAIGN_NAME_SHORT - string, free, mandatory Identifier campaign, foreign key referencing Short campaign name. ENVIRON_COMPARTMENT - string, controlled, mandatory Transcribed Environmental Compartment Samples data. PARAMETER_TYPE - string, controlled, mandatory Transcribed Parameter Type Parameters data table.. MEASURED_TYPE - string, controlled, mandatory type measurement taken sample. Typically concentration, options provided. MEASUREMENT_COMMENT - string, free, optional Space recording additional notes comments sample measurement deemed relevant.

Fractionation protocol: fraction analyte analysed? fraction obtained? Analytical protocol: analytical techniques used determine quantity analyte sample? methodologies structured collection data chemicals stressors, reflects dominant use case format far. Extension cover greater range non-chemical stressors planned future versions. Currently external ontology taxonomy used methodology. text: PARAMETER_TYPE - string, controlled, mandatory Parameter type divided five classifications two missing data values. classification requires degree subjective judgement. makes chemical stressor versus quality parameter always clear. However, anticipate cases uncontroversial. case stressors can still identified name/id. "relevant" - Use type applicable measured scalar. "Stressor" - Chemical, physical biological agent causing stress. "Quality parameter" - Water/environmental quality parameter. "Normalisation" - Parameter used normalising measurements. "Background" - Natural background level parameter. "Ecological Indicator" - Parameter indicator metric ecological concern. "" - Parameter belongs another category. Please note comments field. PARAMETER_TYPE_SUB - string, controlled, mandatory Parameter Sub-type subdivision Parameter Type. majority subtypes chemical classifications ChemOnt/ClassyFire chemical taxonomy; subtypes intended use quality parameters, stressors measured mixtures, cases additional details needed. Existing parameters format already assigned subtype, new parameters assigned relevant subtype. PARAMETER_NAME - string, controlled, mandatory Parameter name records reported name measured parameter. majority cases short-form conventional chemical name; , taken ChemOnt/ClassyFire. maximise standardisation recommend use existing available names rather entering new ones; may require manual validation CAS codes, InChIKeys, etc. Parameter name currently used primary key Parameters data table foreign key Samples Measurements tables. PARAMETER_NAME_SUB - string, controlled, optional cases may necessarily identify parameters finer level detail permitted fields - example, individual wavelengths ionising radiation. case Parameter Sub-Name field used; otherwise can left blank. INCHIKEY_SD - string, free, optional IUPAC International Chemical Identifier (InChI) widely used chemical identifier based molecular structure chemical. InChIKey 27-character string letters seperator characters (e.g., ethanol: LFQSCWFLJHTTHZ-UHFFFAOYSA-N) become gold-standard chemical identifier. parameters chemicals, field left optional, chemicals quantified, strongly preferred InChIKey made available. PUBCHEM_CID - integer, free, optional PubChem database uses internal identifier known PubChem Compound Identifier. integer accession number. InChIKeys strongly preferred identification, PubChem CID can included desired cross-referencing. CAS_RN- string, free, optional Americal Chemical Society maintains extensive registry chemicals. CAS Registry Numbers widely used identify individual chemicals. cases, single substance may one CAS, substances CAS assigned. InChIKeys strongly preferred identification, CAS RN can included desired cross-referencing. ENTERED_BY - string, free, mandatory name email address user entering data. many parameters included part format, needed new parameter added. PARAMETER_COMMENT - string, free, optional Space recording additional notes comments parameter deemed relevant.

title: FAQ
text: data typically published concentrations (water) mass ratios (soil, sludge biota). Fully interpreting measurements requires considerable metadata, relating time, place, conditions, methodology, context sampling. However, single standard exists metadata, study authors sampling teams tend use variety inconsistent definitions, units, schema depending scientific domain study’s individual needs. , problem. However, environmental risk assessment becomes data-based larger scale, need use re-use large quantities data increases. datasets consistent, validated, interoperable, much time can saved. , researchers much go tedious process understanding, validating remapping hand; often non-reproducible process. Thus harmonised format - associated validation formatting functions - can represent significant timesaver anyone position.

title: Reference data

code: library(eDataDRF) initialise_references_tibble() #> # A tibble: 0 × 18 #> # ℹ 18 variables: REFERENCE_ID <chr>, REFERENCE_TYPE <chr>, DATA_SOURCE <chr>, #> #   AUTHOR <chr>, TITLE <chr>, YEAR <int>, ACCESS_DATE <date>, #> #   PERIODICAL_JOURNAL <chr>, VOLUME <int>, ISSUE <int>, PUBLISHER <chr>, #> #   INSTITUTION <chr>, DOI <chr>, URL <chr>, ISBN_ISSN <chr>, EDITION <chr>, #> #   DOCUMENT_NUMBER <chr>, REF_COMMENT <chr>

title: Reference data
text: REFERENCE_ID - string, auto-generated, mandatory reference’s primary key, automatically generated YEAR, AUTHOR, TITLE using generate_reference_id(), follows: Reference ID also foreign key Measurement data CREED scores. REFERENCE_TYPE - string, controlled, mandatory type publication. Currently restricted Journal Article, Book, Report, Dataset/Database. AUTHOR - string, free, mandatory full authors list referenced object, formatted Lastname1, Firstname1; Lastname2, Firstname2; etc.. TITLE - string, free, mandatory title publication document. YEAR - integer, free, mandatory year publication recent update referenced object. ACCESS_DATE - date (ISO), free, mandatory ISO date referenced object accessed. PERIODICAL_JOURNAL - string, free, conditional reference type name journal periodical journal article published . Conditionally mandatory Reference Type Journal Article. VOLUME - numeric, free, optional volume named journal article published . Optional. ISSUE - numeric, free, optional issue named journal article published . Optional. PUBLISHER - string, free, conditional reference type publisher reference object. Conditionally mandatory Reference Type Book Report. INSTITUTION - string, free, conditional reference type primary instition responsible referenced object. Conditionally mandatory Reference Type Report. DOI - String, free, optional available, Digital Object Identifier associated reference object. Optional, preferred identifier available. See website doi Foundation information. URL - String, free, optional Uniform Resource Locator (URL, .e. web address) linking referenced object, available. Optional. ISBN_ISSN - String, free, optional International Standard Book Number (ISBN, 10 13 digits) International Standard Serial Number (ISSN, 8 digits) associated book periodical. Optional. EDITION - String, free, optional edition referenced object, relevant. Optional. DOCUMENT_NUMBER - String, free, optional document report number, typically available reports official documents. Optional. REF_COMMENT - String, free, optional Space recording additional notes comments reference deemed relevant.
code: generate_reference_id(   date = 2018, # i.e. publication date   author = "Last1, First1; Last2, First2", # Take last name of first author   title = "A study of recent developments in the field" # Extract first three words, convert to PascalCase ) #> [1] "2018Last1AStudyOf"

Depending stressors interest, may also case sites, dates, etc. reported source relevant extraction. case, capturing relevant sample combinations often challenging previous modules. However, important step prevent Measurements data table filled invalid combinations never actually sampled. Samples data table references Sites data (via Site code), Parameter data (via Parameter name) Compartments data (via Environmental compartment, Environmental sub-compartment, Measured category). three foreign keys combined relevant Sampling dates (entered manually), potentially, Subsamples, generate full listing relevant samples. particular, Subsample variable allows recording cases sampling may repeated (replicates), single sample may split analysis (sediment cores, biota tissue, etc.).

title: Sites data

Sampling sites often reported variety --less FAIR formats, despite availability free, mature, well-characterised systems defining position space. Extraction data reference objects may require conversion minute-degrees decimal degrees, reprojection different Coordinate Reference System, merging multiple sampling sites, reporting large, vague sampling region single point, estimating coordinates maps. However, time , format kept intentionally simple. sites reported discrete points; cases operations performed reported comments field.

title: Sites data
text: SITE_CODE short, unique code identifying sampled site. Although formatting enforced field, recommend using abbreviated combination campaign, long form site name details relevant site. example: “Arctic Monitoring Program; Pelagic Sampling Point 37” -> AMP-Pelagic-37. site code primary key sites data used foreign key Samples Measurements data tables. SITE_NAME longer form name site, potentially including details address, region, site type, etc. May fully copied source enable easy tracing back relevant details. SITE_GEOGRAPHIC_FEATURE Categorisation site type allows broad comparisons made. Site Geographic feature based variety existing land-use taxonomy facilitate conversion comparison: LUCAS Land Use Cover Hierachy (EU) CORINE Land Cover inventory (EU) Water Framework Directive water body types Marine Something Framework Directive water types summarised table SITE_GEOGRAPHIC_FEATURE_SUB cases geographic feature vocabulary may enough resolution capture sampling sites. example, bodies water, may important distinguish samples benthos, water column, water surface, etc. case, sub-features can used add additional precision. COUNTRY_ISO Although sheer size countries makes times poor identifiers geographical location, remain useful data. Although variable marked mandatory, possible enter “relevant” sites territory nation (e.g. international waters). ISO 3166 set country codes used, via R package {iso}. OCEAN_IHO countries, oceans necessarily best geographical identifiers, provide useful, human-readable data understanding sampling sites. International Hydrographic Organisation’s version 3 Sea Areas dataset used provide reasonably-standardised set names seas oceans. ISO Country, Ocean mandatory marked “relevant” site land. sampling sites located national territory also near (intentionally vague) ocean sea, recommend recording variables. LATITUDE Site latitude (north-south position, also known northing, -90.0° +90.0°) recorded decimal decimal degrees. Arbitrary precision (within bounds R’s numeric data type) possible, degree precision reported (.e. decimal places) appropriate actual sampling. 3 decimal places corresponds approximately 111 m accuracy, 4 within 11 m. circumstances can cause somewhat confusing combinations coordinates site types; example, site may reported land coordinates clearly ocean. future, plan include explicit metric spatial uncertainty. latitude always associated Coordinate Reference System. LONGITUDE Site longitude (east-west position, also known easting, -180.0° +180.0°) recorded decimal decimal degrees. See Latitude details. longitude always associated Coordinate Reference System. SITE_COORDINATE_SYSTEM site’s latitude longitude calculated using reference system “placed” given point globe parameterised within given region. Coordinate Reference System necessary component geographical coordinate data. global datasets, World Geodetic System, 1984 version (WGS 84) used. However, national local datasets may use variety CRS, Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM), projects Earth series square rectangular zones; case, necessary know CRS grid number. controlled vocabulary uses package {crsuggest}, turn uses EPSG Dataset v10.019, (product International Association Oil & Gas Producers, terms use). However, full 6000+ CRS included impractically large associated UI, filter small set systems relevant test cases used format far. future, CRS metadata used automatically filter available CRS based desired geographical scope. ALTITUDE_VALUE Altitude value sea level, including depth water sampling (depth ice sediment cores). data frequently available sampling sites, case can reported 0. Associated altitude unit. ALTITUDE_UNIT Unit associated altitude value. ENTERED_BY name email address user entering data. ENTERED_DATE date site data entered. SITE_COMMENT Space recording additional notes comments site deemed relevant, particularly operations performed user sites data, merging sites, converting sampling region discrete point, estimating point coordinates map.

title: Sites data
text: OCEAN_IHO countries, oceans necessarily best geographical identifiers, provide useful, human-readable data understanding sampling sites. International Hydrographic Organisation’s version 3 Sea Areas dataset used provide reasonably-standardised set names seas oceans. ISO Country, Ocean mandatory marked “relevant” site land. sampling sites located national territory also near (intentionally vague) ocean sea, recommend recording variables.

title: Sites data
text: LATITUDE Site latitude (north-south position, also known northing, -90.0° +90.0°) recorded decimal decimal degrees. Arbitrary precision (within bounds R’s numeric data type) possible, degree precision reported (.e. decimal places) appropriate actual sampling. 3 decimal places corresponds approximately 111 m accuracy, 4 within 11 m. circumstances can cause somewhat confusing combinations coordinates site types; example, site may reported land coordinates clearly ocean. future, plan include explicit metric spatial uncertainty. latitude always associated Coordinate Reference System.

title: Sites data
text: LONGITUDE Site longitude (east-west position, also known easting, -180.0° +180.0°) recorded decimal decimal degrees. See Latitude details. longitude always associated Coordinate Reference System.

what: Coordinate Reference System - String, controlled, mandatory
title: Sites data
text: SITE_COORDINATE_SYSTEM site’s latitude longitude calculated using reference system “placed” given point globe parameterised within given region. Coordinate Reference System necessary component geographical coordinate data. global datasets, World Geodetic System, 1984 version (WGS 84) used. However, national local datasets may use variety CRS, Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM), projects Earth series square rectangular zones; case, necessary know CRS grid number. controlled vocabulary uses package {crsuggest}, turn uses EPSG Dataset v10.019, (product International Association Oil & Gas Producers, terms use). However, full 6000+ CRS included impractically large associated UI, filter small set systems relevant test cases used format far. future, CRS metadata used automatically filter available CRS based desired geographical scope.

returns a nested list environ_compartments_sub_vocabulary()[1]  #> $Aquatic #>                    Freshwater             Marine/Salt Water  #>                  "Freshwater"           "Marine/Salt Water"  #>   Brackish/Transitional Water                   Groundwater  #> "Brackish/Transitional Water"                 "Groundwater"  #>                    Wastewater          Liquid Growth Medium  #>                  "Wastewater"        "Liquid Growth Medium"  #>                     Rainwater                    Stormwater  #>                   "Rainwater"                  "Stormwater"  #>                      Leachate              Aquatic Sediment  #>                    "Leachate"            "Aquatic Sediment"  #>                     Porewater                        Sludge  #>                   "Porewater"                      "Sludge"  #>                      Snow/Ice  #>                    "Snow/Ice"  # returns a tibble extraction_protocols_vocabulary()[1:5, 3]  #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   Long_Name                  #>   <chr>                      #> 1 Not relevant               #> 2 Not reported               #> 3 No extraction              #> 4 Methanol extraction        #> 5 Dichloromethane extraction  # calls bind_rows() on four *_protocol_vocabulary() functions to return a tibble protocol_options_vocabulary()[1:5, 3]  #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   Long_Name          #>   <chr>              #> 1 Not relevant       #> 2 Not reported       #> 3 Point sampling     #> 4 Composite sampling #> 5 Trawl sampling  # what: Generic function to abbreviate string to first n words with case formatting — abbreviate_string
title: Generic function to abbreviate string to first n words with case formatting — abbreviate_string
text: Takes string, extracts first n words (removing special characters), formats according specified case style.

title: Generic function to abbreviate string to first n words with case formatting — abbreviate_string
Generic function to abbreviate string to first n words with case formatting — abbreviate_string
text: string Character. input string abbreviate. n_words Integer. Number words include abbreviation. case Character. Case style output. One : "lower" — lowercase, separator (e.g., "dogsandcats") "upper" — uppercase, separator (e.g., "DOGSANDCATS") "sentence" — sentence case, separator (e.g., "Dogsandcats") "snake" — lowercase underscores (e.g., "dogs_and_cats") "title" — title case, separator (e.g., "DogsAndCats") "screamingsnake" — uppercase underscores (e.g., "DOGS_AND_CATS") "camel" — camel case (e.g., "dogsAndCats")

title: Generic function to abbreviate string to first n words with case formatting — abbreviate_string
text: Character. abbreviated formatted string.

title: Generic function to abbreviate string to first n words with case formatting — abbreviate_string
code: abbreviate_string("Total Phosphorus Concentration", n_words = 2L, "snake") #> [1] "total_phosphorus" abbreviate_string("dogs and cats", n_words = 3L, "title") #> [1] "DogsAndCats" abbreviate_string("Water Quality Index", n_words = 3L, "camel") #> [1] "waterQualityIndex"

what: Export Multiple Datasets as ZIP of CSV Files — export_campaign_zip
title: Export Multiple Datasets as ZIP of CSV Files — export_campaign_zip
text: Creates ZIP archive containing CSV files non-empty dataset provided list. Useful bundling campaign data download archival.

title: Export Multiple Datasets as ZIP of CSV Files — export_campaign_zip
Export Multiple Datasets as ZIP of CSV Files — export_campaign_zip
text: dataset_list Named list tibbles/data.frames export. Names match expected dataset names (e.g., "edata_sites", "edata_measurements"). Empty datasets (NULL, 0 rows) automatically filtered . campaign_name Character. Campaign identifier used ZIP filename individual CSV filenames. Special characters replaced underscores. Default "Unknown_Campaign". output_path Character. Full path ZIP file saved. NULL (default), saves current working directory auto-generated name.

title: Export Multiple Datasets as ZIP of CSV Files — export_campaign_zip
text: Character. Path created ZIP file (returned invisibly).

title: Export Multiple Datasets as ZIP of CSV Files — export_campaign_zip
text: function: Filters empty NULL datasets Creates timestamped filenames reproducibility Writes CSVs Excel-compatible encoding Cleans temporary files automatically Provides progress messages export ZIP filename format: {campaign_name}_{timestamp}.zip CSV filename format: {campaign_name}_{dataset_name}_{timestamp}.csv

title: Export Multiple Datasets as ZIP of CSV Files — export_campaign_zip
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create sample datasets my_data <- list(   edata_sites = tibble(SITE_CODE = "S01", SITE_NAME = "Site 1"),   edata_measurements = tibble(VALUE = c(1.2, 3.4)) )  # Export to ZIP zip_path <- export_campaign_zip(my_data, "MyStudy2024")  # Export to specific location export_campaign_zip(my_data, "MyStudy", "~/Downloads/export.zip") } # }

what: Generate a semi-unique string ID for a protocol based on its type, name, the campaign name, and a sequence number; used as a key between Methods and Measurements table. — generate_protocol_id
title: Generate a semi-unique string ID for a protocol based on its type, name, the campaign name, and a sequence number; used as a key between Methods and Measurements table. — generate_protocol_id
text: Generates standardised protocol identifier based protocol type, name, sequence number, campaign. function fully vectorised can handle multiple protocols simultaneously.

title: Generate a semi-unique string ID for a protocol based on its type, name, the campaign name, and a sequence number; used as a key between Methods and Measurements table. — generate_protocol_id
Generate a semi-unique string ID for a protocol based on its type, name, the campaign name, and a sequence number; used as a key between Methods and Measurements table. — generate_protocol_id
text: protocol_type Character vector. category protocol (e.g., "Sampling Protocol", "Analytical Protocol"). Must match one predefined categories default "X". protocol_name Character vector. specific name/method within protocol category. abbreviated cleaned ID generation. sequence_number Numeric vector. Sequential number protocols within category. Defaults 1. zero-padded 2 digits. campaign_name Character vector. Name campaign/study. abbreviated first 10 alphanumeric characters. Defaults "".

title: Generate a semi-unique string ID for a protocol based on its type, name, the campaign name, and a sequence number; used as a key between Methods and Measurements table. — generate_protocol_id
text: Character vector protocol IDs format: TypeCodeSequenceNumber_AbbreviatedName_CampaignAbbrev : TypeCode: S (Sampling), F (Fractionation), E (Extraction), (Analytical), X (Unknown) SequenceNumber: Zero-padded 2-digit number AbbreviatedName: 15 alphanumeric characters (optional) CampaignAbbrev: 10 alphanumeric characters (optional)

title: Generate a semi-unique string ID for a protocol based on its type, name, the campaign name, and a sequence number; used as a key between Methods and Measurements table. — generate_protocol_id
text: function handles edge cases gracefully: NULL empty values result appropriate defaults Invalid protocol types default "X" Names cleaned special characters spaces Empty components omitted final ID

title: Generate a semi-unique string ID for a protocol based on its type, name, the campaign name, and a sequence number; used as a key between Methods and Measurements table. — generate_protocol_id
code: # Single protocol generate_protocol_id("Sampling Protocol", "Water Sample", 1, "MyStudy") #> [1] "S01_WaterSample_MyStudy" # Returns: "S01_WaterSample_MyStudy"  # Multiple protocols (vectorised) types <- c("Sampling Protocol", "Analytical Protocol") names <- c("Water Sample", "LC-MS Analysis") sequences <- c(1, 2) generate_protocol_id(types, names, sequences, "Study2024") #> [1] "S01_WaterSample_Study2024"  "A02_LCMSAnalysis_Study2024" # Returns: c("S01_WaterSample_Study2024", "A02_LCMSAnalysis_Study2024")

title: Generate compact Sample ID (SiteCode-ParamAbbrev-CompartmentAbbrev-Date-R-SubsampleAbbrev) for SAMPLE_ID in Samples table — generate_sample_id_with_components
text: site_code Character vector. site code (e.g. "SITE-001"). parameter_name Character vector. parameter name. abbreviated first 8 alphanumeric characters. environ_compartment Character vector. environmental compartment (e.g. "Aquatic"). used ID directly retained parameter future use documentation purposes. environ_compartment_sub Character vector. environmental sub-compartment (e.g. "Aquatic Sediment"). abbreviated first 12 alphanumeric characters. date Character Date vector. sampling date (e.g. "2023-03-15"). Used -ID. subsample Character numeric vector. subsample label. abbreviated first 3 words title case. Defaults 1.

title: Initialise an 14x0 tibble for storing biota metadata — initialise_biota_tibble
text: Biota data extends sample information species-specific details including taxonomic classification, tissue type, life stage, gender information. tibble includes following columns: SAMPLE_ID: Unique identifier linking samples table (mandatory, foreign key measurements) SITE_CODE: Code identifying sampling location (mandatory, foreign key sites) PARAMETER_NAME: Name measured parameter stressor (mandatory, foreign key parameters) ENVIRON_COMPARTMENT: Broad environmental compartment category (mandatory, inherited samples) ENVIRON_COMPARTMENT_SUB: Specific sub-category environmental compartment (mandatory, inherited samples) MEASURED_CATEGORY: Category indicating measurement context (mandatory, inherited samples) SAMPLING_DATE: Date biological sample collected (mandatory, inherited samples) SUBSAMPLE: Identifier subsample replicate (mandatory, inherited samples) SPECIES_GROUP: Taxonomic group classification, e.g., Fish, Molluscs, Plants (mandatory) SAMPLE_SPECIES: Scientific binomial name sampled species (mandatory) SAMPLE_TISSUE: Type biological tissue sampled (mandatory) SAMPLE_SPECIES_LIFESTAGE: Life stage organism sampling (mandatory) SAMPLE_SPECIES_GENDER: Gender sex sampled organism (mandatory) BIOTA_COMMENT: Additional notes comments biological sample (optional) See vignette("biota_data") information.

title: Initialise an 14x0 tibble for storing biota metadata — initialise_biota_tibble
code: initialise_biota_tibble() #> # A tibble: 0 × 14 #> # ℹ 14 variables: SAMPLE_ID <chr>, SITE_CODE <chr>, PARAMETER_NAME <chr>, #> #   ENVIRON_COMPARTMENT <chr>, ENVIRON_COMPARTMENT_SUB <chr>, #> #   MEASURED_CATEGORY <chr>, SAMPLING_DATE <chr>, SUBSAMPLE <chr>, #> #   SPECIES_GROUP <chr>, SAMPLE_SPECIES <chr>, SAMPLE_TISSUE <chr>, #> #   SAMPLE_SPECIES_LIFESTAGE <chr>, SAMPLE_SPECIES_GENDER <chr>, #> #   BIOTA_COMMENT <chr>

title: Controlled vocabulary for MEASURED_UNIT, LOQ_UNIT, and LOD_UNIT, with conversion factors and base units — parameter_unit_vocabulary
text: Reads unit conversion data unit_conversion_factors.csv containing standardised units ecotoxicological environmental measurements. complete dataset includes columns: MEASURED_UNIT: Unit recorded measurements BASE_SI_UNIT: Corresponding SI base unit CONVERSION_FACTOR: Numeric factor convert SI units UNIT_COMMENTS: Additional notes unit usage conversion Data source: extdata/unit_conversion_factors.csv

title: Controlled vocabulary for MEASURED_UNIT, LOQ_UNIT, and LOD_UNIT, with conversion factors and base units — parameter_unit_vocabulary
code: head(parameter_unit_vocabulary(), 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   MEASURED_UNIT BASE_SI_UNIT CONVERSION_FACTOR UNIT_COMMENTS       #>   <chr>         <chr>        <chr>             <chr>               #> 1 mg/L          kg/m³        1e-3              mass concentration  #> 2 μg/L          kg/m³        1e-6              mass concentration  #> 3 ng/L          kg/m³        1e-9              mass concentration  #> 4 pg/L          kg/m³        1e-12             mass concentration  #> 5 mol/L         mol/m³       1000              molar concentration parameter_unit_vocabulary(select_column = "MEASURED_UNIT") |> head(5) #> [1] "mg/L"  "μg/L"  "ng/L"  "pg/L"  "mol/L"

text: Applies pointblank validation rules supplied eData tables calling individual table validation functions. Required tables always validated; optional tables validated provided.

title: Validate all eData tables at once — pb_validate_all_edata_tables
code: pb_validate_all_edata_tables(   campaign,   reference,   parameters,   sites,   samples = NULL,   biota = NULL,   measurements,   methods = NULL,   creed_scores = NULL,   actions = action_levels(),   agent = TRUE,   northern_hemisphere = FALSE )

title: Validate all eData tables at once — pb_validate_all_edata_tables
text: campaign Data frame containing Campaign table data validate reference Data frame containing Reference table data validate parameters Data frame containing Parameters table data validate sites Data frame containing Sites table data validate samples Data frame containing Samples table data validate (optional) biota Data frame containing Biota table data validate (optional) measurements Data frame containing Measurements table data validate methods Data frame containing Methods table data validate (optional) creed_scores Data frame containing CREED Scores table data validate (optional) actions Action levels pointblank agents (used agent = TRUE) agent Logical. TRUE (default), returns list pointblank agent objects. FALSE, returns list validated data frames failures removed. northern_hemisphere Logical. TRUE, check site coordinates northern hemisphere. Passed pb_validate_sites().

title: Validate all eData tables at once — pb_validate_all_edata_tables
text: named list. agent = TRUE, element pointblank agent object. agent = FALSE, element validated data frame failing rows removed. list always contains elements named campaign, reference, parameters, sites, measurements. Optional elements (samples, biota, methods, creed_scores) included corresponding arguments NULL.

title: Validate all eData tables at once — pb_validate_all_edata_tables
text: Calls individual table validation function turn. Validation performed using pointblank::create_agent() related functions. See vignette("validation", package = "pointblank") overview pointblank validation workflow.

title: Validate all eData tables at once — pb_validate_all_edata_tables
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ agents <- pb_validate_all_edata_tables(   campaign     = example_campaign_tibble(),   reference    = example_references_tibble(),   parameters   = example_parameters_tibble(),   sites        = example_sites_tibble(),   measurements = example_measurements_tibble() )  # Include optional tables agents <- pb_validate_all_edata_tables(   campaign     = example_campaign_tibble(),   reference    = example_references_tibble(),   parameters   = example_parameters_tibble(),   sites        = example_sites_tibble(),   measurements = example_measurements_tibble(),   samples      = example_samples_tibble(),   biota        = example_biota_tibble(),   methods      = example_methods_tibble(),   creed_scores = example_CREED_scores_tibble() ) } # }

title: Run pointblank validation on an eData table — pb_validate_edata_table
text: data Data frame validate table_name Name table validated (reporting) validation_steps Function takes data frame agent returns validation steps added. function like: function(x) { x |> col_vals_not_null(...) |> ... } agent Logical. TRUE (default), returns pointblank agent object. FALSE, returns validated data validation failures removed. actions Action levels pointblank agent (used agent = TRUE)

title: Regular expression to check generate_protocol_id() protocol IDs for Methods table — protocol_id_regex
text: pattern matches format TypeCodeSequenceNumber_AbbreviatedName_CampaignAbbrev produced generate_protocol_id(), : TypeCode: one S, F, E, , X SequenceNumber: exactly two digits (zero-padded) _AbbreviatedName: optional underscore + 15 alphanumeric characters _CampaignAbbrev: optional underscore + 10 alphanumeric characters Examples valid IDs: S01_WaterSample_MyStudy, A02_LCMS_Study2024

Data read ecotox_2025_06_12_species.parquet. Additional entries "" "Ecosystem" included. Data retrieved https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/, 2025.06.12.
